payload.text:
You are an expert Full-Stack Dashboard Engineer.

Create a NEW fully working dashboard app based on a PUBLIC Google Sheet.
The app must be accurate, optimized, and error-free.

========================================================
STEP 1 — DEFAULT SETTINGS
========================================================

Default sheet name = "Sheet1"
(User can change it later in settings menu.)

Ask user only for:
1. Google Sheet URL
2. Optional sheet name (default = Sheet1)
3. Optional range

========================================================
STEP 2 — COLUMN DETECTION
========================================================

Auto-detect column headers.

Required columns (case-insensitive):

UID
RING STATUS
DATE
BATCH NO
INWARD
SKU
REASON   ← for rejection details chart

If any column missing → show warning + allow remap.

========================================================
STEP 3 — FILTER PANEL
========================================================

Top filter section.

FILTER 1 — ADVANCED DATE FILTER
Column: DATE
Include:
• Start & End picker
• Presets: Today / Last 7 Days / Last 30 Days / This Month
• Auto-detect text dates
Default → All data.

FILTER 2 — BATCH FILTER
Column: BATCH NO
Type → Dropdown Checkbox
Include:
✔ Select All
✔ Deselect All
Default → All selected.

All KPIs & charts MUST use filtered dataset only.

Always:
FILTER → CALCULATE → DISPLAY

========================================================
STEP 4 — KPI LOGIC
========================================================

After filters applied:

TOTAL =
Count non-empty UID

ACCEPTED =
Count rows where RING STATUS = "Accepted"

REJECTED =
Count rows where RING STATUS = "Rejected"

WIP =
Count non-empty INWARD − Count non-empty UID

YIELD =
(Accepted / Total) × 100

Rules:
• Ignore blanks
• Case-insensitive match
• Trim spaces
• If WIP negative → show 0
• Recalculate on filter change
• No cached data

========================================================
STEP 5 — CHARTS
========================================================

CHART 1 — Accepted SKU Count
Filter rows where RING STATUS = "Accepted"
Group by SKU
Count SKU
Bar color metallic green.

CHART 2 — Rejected SKU Count
Filter rows where RING STATUS = "Rejected"
Group by SKU
Count SKU
Bar color metallic red.

CHART 3 — Rejection Details Chart
Column: REASON
From filtered dataset where RING STATUS = "Rejected"
Group by REASON
Count occurrences.

Show as bar or pie chart titled:
"Rejection Details by Reason"

========================================================
STEP 6 — HAMBURGER MENU
========================================================

Add ☰ settings menu with:

• Google Sheet URL
• Sheet selector (default Sheet1)
• Range selector
• Column mapping
• Refresh button

Changes reload dashboard instantly.

========================================================
STEP 7 — ERROR PREVENTION
========================================================

Before showing dashboard:

✔ Check sheet is public
✔ Check Sheet1 exists
✔ Check columns exist
✔ Remove blank rows
✔ Fix date format automatically
✔ Check filters not removing all rows
✔ Show message if no data
✔ Never mix sheets
✔ No wrong aggregation

========================================================
STEP 8 — LAYOUT
========================================================

Row 1 → Filters  
Row 2 → KPI Cards  
Row 3 → SKU Charts  
Row 4 → Rejection Details Chart  

Clean modern responsive UI.

**Defining Project Scope**

I'm solidifying the project's parameters. Right now, I'm focused on developing a React application with full-stack capabilities to dynamically display data from a public Google Sheet. The application will be an interactive dashboard. I am establishing how the Google Sheet will feed into the dashboard, focusing on the essential inputs needed, like the URL, sheet name, and the specific data range. I'm also planning to incorporate a column mapping feature for data transformation.


**Outlining Data Requirements**

I'm now detailing the application's specific data requirements and functionalities. Filtering will include date range and batch number multi-select. I am planning key performance indicators (KPIs) like totals, accepted, and rejected items, plus work in progress and yield. I'll visualize accepted and rejected SKU counts with bar charts, and rejection details with a pie or bar chart. The settings will be in a hamburger menu, and I am planning to validate and clean the data. I've chosen CSV export as the primary data source to avoid complex API keys. I will develop using React 18+ and TypeScript, with Tailwind CSS, `lucide-react`, and `recharts`.


**Mapping Out Components**

I've been mapping out the application's core components and state management strategy. I'll utilize `date-fns` and `papaparse` for date manipulation and CSV parsing. My main components include `Layout`, `FilterPanel`, `KPICards`, `Charts`, and `SettingsModal`, while the data will be managed through `config`, `rawData`, `filteredData`, and `mapping` states. I've also outlined the project's file structure. I am working on parsing Google Sheet URLs.


**Refining Technical Specifications**

I'm now detailing technical implementation specifics. I'm focusing on crafting a column mapping system with a default identity. I've also defined the KPI logic using JavaScript array methods, including calculations for Total, Accepted, Rejected, WIP, and Yield. The UI is planned with a modern look using metallic green and red. I'll include loading states and error banners. I'm planning to check for public sheet availability, and I'll robustly parse dates.
